response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack/web engineer with broad language exposure, but the role requires specific embedded/networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, switch chips, Linux kernel modification) and native-level Japanese — none of which appear in the resume. C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago, and there is no evidence of firmware, switch SDK, or kernel-level work. The language requirement alone is a hard blocker.
strengths: Long track record of shipping production software across multiple stacks
Some historical C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a developer IDE
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing, workshops
concerns: No native-level (or any) Japanese proficiency listed
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, MACsec, 802.1X, or kernel-modification experience
C is not a current core skill; recent work is TypeScript/Node and DLang

key_factors.factor: General software engineering depth and breadth
